XPhysicalProtect

The XPhysicalProtect function changes the access protection on a region of physical memory allocated with the XPhysicalAlloc function.

VOID XPhysicalProtect(
  LPVOID lpAddress,   // address of physical memory
  SIZE_T dwSize,      // size of the region
  DWORD flNewProtect  // desired access protection
);

Parameters

lpAddress
[in] Pointer to the base address of the region of physical memory where the access protection attributes are to be changed.

All pages in the specified region must be within a memory block allocated with a single call to XPhysicalAlloc. The pages cannot span adjacent regions allocated by separate calls to XPhysicalAlloc.

dwSize
[in] Specifies the size, in bytes, of the region whose access protection attributes are to be changed. The region of affected pages includes all pages containing one or more bytes in the range from the lpAddress parameter to (lpAddress+dwSize). This means that a 2-byte range straddling a page boundary causes the protection attributes of both pages to be changed.
flNewProtect
[in] Specifies the new access protection and optional memory type modifiers for the region of pages. One of the following access protection flags must be specified:
Value Meaning
PAGE_READONLY Enables read and execute access to the committed region of pages. An attempt to write to the committed region results in an access violation.
PAGE_READWRITE Enables read, write, and execute access to the committed region of pages.

In addition, one of the following flags may be specified to modify the characteristics of the memory:

Value Meaning
PAGE_NOCACHE Allows no memory caching for memory in the committed region of pages. The hardware attributes for the physical memory will be treated as "no cache." This is not recommended for general usage. It is useful for device drivers; for example, mapping a video frame buffer with no caching.
PAGE_WRITECOMBINE The allocated memory has a write-combining cache policy. This is not recommended for general usage. It is useful for device drivers; for example, mapping a video frame buffer. Write combining is a special caching mode that batches writes to the same cache line so they can be transferred in a single bus clock. Write combining does not preserve ordering of the writes, a tradeoff that is usually acceptable for frame buffers.

Return Values

This function does not return a value.

Requirements

Header: Declared in Winbase.h, for include use Xtl.h.
Import Library: Use Xapilib.lib

See Also

Memory Management Functions | XPhysicalAlloc, VirtualProtect
